title: Complete the implementation

content.problemStatement:
Given a quickSort function that accepts an array as a parameter, sort the array using the **Quick sort** algorithm.

Video Explanation
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of elements in the array. The second line contains N space-separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the N integers of the sorted array in non‑decreasing order, separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each test case is sorted independently using Quick Sort, producing the arrays in ascending order.
A single element array is already sorted; the algorithm returns it unchanged.
The descending array is reordered into ascending order after the recursive partition steps.

solution.approach: We implement the classic Quick Sort algorithm. The last element of the current sub‑array is chosen as the pivot. Elements smaller than the pivot are moved to its left and larger ones to its right using a partition routine. The algorithm is then applied recursively to the left and right partitions. Quick Sort runs in O(N log N) average time and O(N^2) worst‑case, which is acceptable for the given constraints. The recursion depth is O(log N) on average, giving O(log N) auxiliary space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case on average, O(N^2) worst case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(log N) auxiliary stack space due to recursion.
